title: Alcantarea regina
intro: Alcantarea regina, also known as the Queen's bromeliad, is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, characterized by its striking red inflorescence and broad, flat leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Alcantarea regina include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Alcantarea regina is a species of flowering plant in the bromeliad family, Bromeliaceae. It is a type of air plant that is native to Brazil. Alcantarea regina is often classified as a type of epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants or surfaces without deriving nutrients from them. The plant is also known for its striking foliage and vibrant flowers, which can grow up to 2 feet in height. Alcantarea regina is often used as an ornamental plant due to its unique and attractive appearance.

Alcantarea regina has been traditionally used in folk medicine in Brazil, where it is believed to have various health benefits.
